proved: The compiled stdio server advertises 25 semantic tools and routes restore_active_policy_version only to project.restorePolicyVersion.
Restore requires an inspected target version, exact live document revision, exact current non-null immutable head, and caller-supplied attribution.
The MCP bridge reuses the product restoreAutomationPolicyVersion transaction rather than implementing a parallel history path.
The target semantic blocks replace the live draft while a new immutable child is appended to the exact current head; earlier versions remain unchanged.
A synthetic editor failure restores the previous draft and shared head inside the transaction.
The freshly packaged Windows executable checkpointed a temporary divergence, restored the earlier checkpoint, and returned a live readback exactly matching that checkpoint.
Separate stale-document and stale-head packaged calls failed before adding another version.
Bounded research-state inspection remained healthy after checkpoint, divergence, restore, and rejected stale calls.
The complete frontend suite, 25-tool stdio contract, production frontend build, 44-check structural audit, packaged Tauri build, and packaged live mutation passed under explicit deadlines.
The first packaged build failed quickly because llama-server held ggml-base.dll; the exact lock owner was terminated and one bounded retry completed.
notProved: Participant identity is caller supplied and is not authenticated across installations.
Drive or WebSocket restore convergence, network partitions, or two-install behavior.
Crash or power-loss durability during the IndexedDB persistence boundary.
Automatic conflict reconciliation; stale document or head input fails closed and requires a fresh read.
Packaged pointer, keyboard, focus, screen-reader, narrow-layout, or dark-theme restore interaction.
macOS/Linux packaged launch and restore behavior.
Signed updater delivery of this checkpoint.
The live harness intentionally mutated the reusable local MCP pilot project; it did not touch Drive, credentials, or invoke model generation.
